tape: Risk-off deepened slightly on the Iran-US conflict: Iran retaliated against Kuwait, Jordan and Qatar and the IRGC says the Strait of Hormuz is "closed until further notice" (US disputes this), Brent near $80; S&P 500 -0.4%, Nasdaq -1% (chip-led selloff), but VIX is actually down ~5% to 15.03 - no panic, just a mild pullback ahead of Tuesday's CPI print.

alerts.headline: Iran-US conflict widened: Iran struck Kuwait, Jordan and Qatar; IRGC claims Strait of Hormuz closed (US says it remains open). Oil spiking (Brent ~$80), S&P -0.4%, Nasdaq -1%, but VIX down to 15.03 - market not panicking yet. Tail risk live into Tuesday's CPI.

Price data conflict persists: broker shows AVO flat around $13.35 (pnl -0.04), but generic web search again returned a stale/mismatched quote (~$11.50, -3.8%) plus an unverified claim of a completed 'Calavo Growers acquisition' that looks like a search-engine mixup with a different avocado company (Calavo trades as CVGW) - treat that acquisition claim as unconfirmed, not a real catalyst. No credible fresh negative news found. Trust broker price.

Price data conflict continues: broker shows CAI flat/slightly up (~$17.13-17.15), search shows a stale quote of $16.99 (-4.9%) with no corroborating news for a drop - same ticker-ambiguity issue flagged in the prior snapshot. No real catalyst found either way.
+14.4% move continues, unchanged from prior snapshot - still riding the 6/29 BLA filing, Wainwright Buy initiation, and 7/8 director buy ($167K). No new news today.

+4.9% move continues, unchanged from prior snapshot; still no new catalyst since the late-June EVO756 trial miss/downgrades (Morgan Stanley PT cut to $26, Wainwright Neutral). Migraine-prevention Phase IIb pivot (data 2027) already known, not a fresh driver.

Ticker ambiguity blocks reliable search coverage: generic search surfaces Netskope and NetSol Technologies (NTWK) instead of the held NTSK_US_EQ position. No confirmed company-specific news found today.
No new negative news; Equinor Cat-D rig contract and growing backlog (~$7.1B) plus insider buying (7/7) already known from prior snapshots. Benefiting from Hormuz-driven oil spike.

No fresh news; CEO Ross Bhappu's $968K buy (7/7) and the Vacuumschmelze acquisition (6/23) remain the latest known catalysts. Quote seen ~$13.69 in search vs ~$13.18-13.21 on broker - minor feed lag, not a real move.
No new catalyst since the 7/7 WrapShield platform launch and 7/8 director buy; stock still holding the +48% weekly run, move remains extended.

Quiet on watchlist coverage today; a search result surfaced an old (dated ~Nov 2025) 8-K about a CEO/Chairman transition - this is stale, already-priced-in news, not a today event. No fresh news since 5/18 insider buying.
No fresh news found today; 6/24-7/2 offering/PT-hike wave remains the last known catalyst.

movers.note: +14.4%, still riding BLA/coverage/insider-buy momentum, no new news today.
+4.9%, momentum continuing without a new fundamental driver.
Lifted by Hormuz-driven oil spike (Brent ~$80) plus its own backlog growth.
